question_structure.context: Two metal rails are placed parallel to each other on the same horizontal plane, separated by a distance of $l = 0.6 \, \mathrm{m}$. The left and right ends of the rails are connected to lamps $\mathrm{L}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{L}_{2}$ with specifications "3V, 3W" and "6V, 6W" respectively. A conductor rod ab with resistance $\mathbf{r} = 1 \, \Omega$ is placed on the rails and is perpendicular to them. A uniform magnetic field with magnetic induction $\mathrm{B} = 1 \, \mathrm{T}$ passes vertically through the plane of the rails. When the conductor rod moves to the right at a constant speed under the action of an external force, lamp $\mathrm{L}_{1}$ just lights up normally. The resistance of the rails is negligible.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The actual power $\mathrm{P}_{2}$ of the lamp $\mathrm{L}_{2}$.
question_structure.sub_question_2: The speed $v$ of the conductor rod $ab$.
question_structure.sub_question_3: The power of the Ampere force acting on the conductor rod ab $\mathrm{P_{Ampere}}$.

sub_question_1.step_3: The lamp $L_{1}$ is just glowing normally, indicating that the external voltage $U_{\text{out}} = 3 \mathrm{V}$, and the ratio of the actual power of $L_{1}$ to $L_{2}$ is $P_{1} : P_{2}' = R_{2} : R_{1} = 2 : 1$.
sub_question_1.step_4: The actual power of lamp $L_{2}$ is $P_{2}' = 1.5 \mathrm{W}$.

sub_question_2.step_5: The resistance of $L_{1}$ and $L_{2}$ in parallel is $R_{out} = \frac{R_{1} R_{2}}{R_{1} + R_{2}} = 2 \mathrm{\Omega}$.
sub_question_2.step_6: The external and internal currents are equal, so $U_{out}:U_{in} = R_{out}:r = 2:1$
sub_question_2.step_7: The internal voltage $U_{in} = I r = (\frac{U_{1}}{R_{1}} + \frac{U_{1}}{R_{2}}) r = 1.5 \mathrm{V}$
sub_question_2.step_8: The induced electromotive force $E = U_{internal} + U_{external} = 4.5 \mathrm{V}$
sub_question_2.step_10: The speed of the uniform motion of the conductor rod ab is $v = \frac{E}{B L} = 7.5 \mathrm{m/s}$.

sub_question_3.step_11: Since the current in the internal and external circuits is the same, $P_{out}:P_{in} = R_{out}:r = 2:1$
sub_question_3.step_12: The power consumed by the conductor rod is $P_{in} = 2.25 \mathrm{W}$.
sub_question_3.step_13: The power of the Ampere force is equal to the sum of the electrical power consumed by the internal and external circuits, so $P_{a} = P_{1} + P_{2}' + P_{in} = 6.75 \mathrm{W}$.

image_captions: A rectangular circuit with two light bulbs (L1 and L2) on opposite sides is placed in a uniform magnetic field (B) directed into the page. A conducting rod (ab) is moving to the right within the magnetic field, perpendicular to the field lines.

step_3.physical_theorem: Ohm's Law and Power relationship for series/parallel circuits

result_quantity.name: Ratio of actual power of L1 to L2
Equivalent resistance of L1 and L2 in parallel

step_6.physical_theorem: Voltage division in a series circuit

result_quantity.name: Ratio of external voltage to internal voltage

step_8.physical_theorem: Relationship between induced electromotive force and terminal voltage

step_11.physical_theorem: Power distribution in a series circuit

result_quantity.name: Ratio of external power to internal power
Power consumed by the conductor rod
